observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MPDC and Municipal Budget Officer to reconcile their records and submit to the Audit Team a revised 20 percent Utilization Report for CY 2023 and to the oversight agencies, if warranted, to ensure accurate and consistent reporting. We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor ensure the installation, implementation, and monitoring of a sound system of internal control to safeguard its assets, check the accuracy and reliability of its accounting data, and encourage adherence to prescribed managerial policies.
observations.managementAction: No action was taken on the audit recommendation.
observations.reason: No action was taken on the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct: a. The Municipal General Services Officer (MGSO) to prepare the Report on Local Road Network; b. the Municipal Engineer to provide the complete description and cost segregation components for road projects; and c. the Municipal Accountant to prepare a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to record the beginning balance of the local road network and its component in the books based on the Local Road Inventory Report and the cost segregation of road components prepared by the Municipal Engineer; and to keep and maintain subsidiary records for roads and road components for every road network.
observations.managementAction: The MGSO prepared the Report on Local Road Network; the Municipal Engineer provided the description and cost segregation of road components for road projects; the Municipal Accountant committed to prepare the Journal Entry Voucher to record the local road network in the Assets account.
observations.reason: The Report on Local Road Network was not prepared due to inability of the Municipal Engineer to provide the details of the road networks. The complete description and cost segregation of road networks were not yet identified.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer, in
coordination with concerned offices, to stop the practice of granting cash advances with
multiple and varying purposes, in compliance with Sections 89 and 123 of PD No. 1445
and Section 4.1.7 of COA Circular No. 97-002, to safeguard the government fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to formulate
and adopt more aggressive collection strategies, otherwise, consider availing the
remedies provided under Sections 176 and 256 of RA No. 7160 to enforce the collection
and reduction of delinquent real property taxes.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the OIC - Municipal Accountant
to maintain the SPLC, in compliance with Section 4.7.2 of COA Circular No. 2022-004, to
record the acquisition, description, custody, impairment,
issue/transfer/disposal, repair history, and other information about the property.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the OIC - Municipal Accountant,
in coordination with the Municipal General Services Officer (MGSO) and Municipal
Engineer, to verify the validity of payables aged two years and more and pay the
balances to the identified claimants or creditors, otherwise, cause the reversion of
the payables to the unappropriated surplus of the general fund, in compliance with
Section 4(6) and 98 of PD No. 1445, to establish that only valid claims remained in
the AP account.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
a. Municipal General Services Officer and Municipal Engineer to verify the status
of the projects under the Guaranty/Deposit Payable accounts aged more than
one year from the date of retention of money due to them and establish whether
the terms and conditions of the contract are faithfully adhered to by the
concerned suppliers; and
b. OIC–Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to require the concerned
suppliers, after the lapse of the warranty period, to secure the necessary
documents needed to process the refund of their guaranty deposit, in
compliance with Annex B of COA Circular No. 2015-009, Section 62.1 of the 2016
Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 and Section 6.2 of Annex E of the same Revised
IRR, to return the suppliers and contractors’ retention money.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Administrator, in
coordination with concerned officials, to request the copy of signed and notarized
MOA with the various national government agencies, pursuant to Article 1318 of RA
No. 386 and Rule II, Section 7 of the 2004 Rules on Notarial Practice and furnish
copy to the Audit Team for further verification.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain complete subsidiary records of each special account and accordingly, at the end of each year, prepared trial balance and financial statements of each special account, in compliance with Section 313 of RA No. 7160 as reiterated under Sections 107 to 110 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant prepared and maintained complete subsidiary records of each special account and prepared trial balance and financial statements of each special account.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the: a. Municipal General Services Officer (MGSO) to exert extra effort to locate the details and supporting documents and determine the person/s accountable for the missing PPE items and prepare the List of Non-Existing/Missing PPEs for items described as "non-existing" or "missing" in the Report of the Physical Count of PPE (RPCPPE), as required under Sections 6.3.1 and 7.3 of COA Circular No. 2020-006, otherwise, comply the procedures provided under Section 7.11 of the same Circular if accountability could not be established despite exhaustion of all diligent efforts; and b. MGSO, in coordination with the Municipal Accountant and Inventory Committee, to strictly comply with the disposition, derecognition and disposal procedures of all missing and unserviceable PPEs, as required under COA Circular No. 2020-006.
observations.managementAction: The MGSO started the investigation and completion of supporting documents and determine the missing PPEs. The Municipal Accountant provided the necessary disclosures regarding the non-existing/missing PPEs. The MGSO, in coordination with the Municipal Accountant and Inventory Committee, complied with the disposition, derecognition and disposal procedures of all missing and unserviceable PPEs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, in coordination with the Municipal Accountant, to strictly enforce compliance on the granting and utilization of all cash advances, in accordance with COA Circular No. 97-002. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant conducts a thorough review of the details and information of cash advance to be made before granting it.
observations.managementAction: All concerned officers and employees are reminded of their unliquidated cash advance before they become due. The Municipal Accountant conducts a thorough review of the details and information of cash advance to be made before granting it.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct: a. Bids and Awards Committee to adhere with the principles on transparency and competitiveness by conducting public bidding in the procurement of consultancy services, in accordance with Section 10 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184; b. Supervising Administrative Officer to assess the need for the consultants vis-à-vis the availability of regular plantilla personnel that could perform the duties and responsibilities needed to a certain project considering their expertise; and c. Municipal Accountant to ensure the completeness of documentation for the payment of consultancy services, in accordance with Section 9.1.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-001.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality stopped the hiring of Consultants. The Supervising Administrative Officer assessed the need for the consultants vis-à-vis the availability of regular plantilla personnel that could perform the duties and responsibilities. The Municipal Accountant ensured the completeness of documentation for the payment of consultancy services.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the: a. Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer (LDRRMO), in coordination with the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council (LDRRMC), to prepare judiciously the LDRRMFIP by including all the PPAs to be charged under the current year appropriations and the previous years’ unexpended balances, in compliance with Sections 5.1.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-002; b. OIC, Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) to incorporate the LDRRMFIP projects and activities in the local development and annual work and financial plan; c. Municipal Budget Officer to maintain separate RAAOs for Quick Response Fund (QRF), Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses (MOOE) and Capital Outlay, pursuant to Section 5.1.4 of the same Circular; and d. LDRRMO, in coordination with the Municipal Accountant, to prepare and submit the report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF using the format in Annex B on or before the 15th day after the end of each month through the LDRRMC and Local Development Council (LDC) to the Audit Team.
observations.managementAction: The LDRRMFIP was prepared containing the current and previous years’ unexpended balances. The LDRRMF PPAs were incorporated in the AIP of the Municipality during the year. The Statement of Appropriation and Balance (SAAOB) for the QRF, MOOE and CO was presented. The monthly report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF was prepared and submitted using the prescribed format.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the: a. GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) to:  Prepare a more realistic and attainable GPB to ensure that GAD projects will be carried out in accordance with approved plan;  Align the GAD objectives with the Specific, Measurable, Attainable, Relevant and Time-Bound objectives to ensure a more accurate evaluation of agency performances; and  Maximize the utilization of the budget allocated for GAD PPAs in order to attain the objectives for which the funds were provided; b. MPDC, in coordination with GFPS, to establish and maintain GAD Database to serve as basis for gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation; and c. GAD Focal Person, MPDC and Municipal Budget Officer to explain/submit justification on the exceeding of actual expenses from its available appropriation on the PPA entitled “Low awareness of youth on their vital role in nation building and in promoting gender and development” which resulted to overdraft of P313,875.25 for further evaluation of the Audit Team.
observations.managementAction: The GFPS included PPAs that addressed gender issues within the Municipality. The GFPS included PPAs that addressed gender issues within the Municipality. The Municipality had maximized the utilization of GAD Fund. The MPDC, in coordination with the GFPS, will spearhead the formulation and establishment of GAD Database. The Municipal Budget Officer ensured that only eligible PPAs were programmed under the 20 percent DF. The GAD Focal Person, MPDC and Municipal Budget Officer submitted the explanation/justification to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the: a. Municipal Budget Officer to stop the practice of charging the 20 percent DF with ineligible expenses, otherwise, the transactions shall be disallowed in audit; b. Accountable Officer and Municipal Accountant to ensure that the cash advance shall be used solely for the specific legal purpose for which it was granted; and c. MPDC to ensure that development projects included under the 20 percent DF are necessary, appropriate, or incidental to efficient and effective local governance, and those which are essential to the promotion of the general welfare of the people.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Budget Officer ensured that only eligible PPAs were programmed under the 20 percent DF. The Accountable Officer and Municipal Accountant ensured that the cash advance shall be used solely for the specific legal purpose for which it was granted. The MPDC ensured that development projects included under the 20 percent DF are necessary and appropriate.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor to instruct the Inventory and Appraisal Committee, as headed by the MGSO, to fast track the conduct of the one-time cleansing of the PPEs to ascertain the existence, condition and accountability to the PPEs.
observations.managementAction: The One Time Cleansing of PPEs was conducted in CY 2023. The MGSO headed the Inventory and Appraisal Committee and conducted the cleansing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal Assessor to reevaluate or reassess the appraised values of the properties under the Municipality’s jurisdiction based on the prevailing market rates and thereafter make necessary revisions every three years, pursuant to Sections 212 and 219 of RA No. 7160.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Assessor commented that general revision is planned to be undertaken and necessary revisions will be made in accordance with Sections 212 and 219 of RA No. 7160.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor to direct the MHRMO and other concerned personnel to furnish the Audit Team copies of perfected contracts together with its supporting documents within five working days for technical review and evaluation, in compliance with COA Circular No. 2009-001.
observations.managementAction: The MHRMO furnished the Audit Team with copies of perfected contracts together with its supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the: a. Municipal Engineer to:  ensure the preparation of the Program of Works (POWs) before the implementation of the projects as required by Item 3(i) of the Annex “A” of the same Revised IRR; and  ensure preparation and submission of Statement of Work Accomplished (SWA), Inspection Report by the Agency’s Authorized Engineer, Pictures before, during and after construction of certain projects; and b. Municipal Accountant to ensure that the Agency’s disbursements for labor and materials are sufficiently supported of POWs, SWA, Inspection Report by the Agency’s Authorized Engineer, Pictures before, during and after construction of items of work especially the embedded items and Certificate of Completion to support the disbursements, as required in Item 9.1.1 of the COA Circular No. 2012-001.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer prepared POWs to certain projects. The Municipal Engineer prepared SWA, Inspection Reports and Pictures before, during and after construction of certain projects. The Municipal Accountant attached POWs, SWA, Inspection Reports, Pictures and Certificate of Completion to certain disbursements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MPDC and Municipal Budget Officer to prepare the itemized appropriations instead of by lumpsum in the budgets of LGU instead of by lumpsum which specific term which specific projects/activities are embodied in the local development plan and prioritized by the Local Development Council and approved by the Sanggunian concerned, pursuant to Section 4 (1) of the PD No. 1445 and Article 454 (d) of the AO No. 270.
observations.managementAction: The infrastructure projects to be implemented in various barangays of the Municipality were identified and reprogrammed. The Municipal Budget Officer prepared itemized appropriations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office (MDRRMO), in coordination with the LDRRMC, to prepare the LDRRMFIP by including the PPAs funded by the current year appropriations and the previous year’s unexpended balances in compliance with Section 5.1.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.
observations.managementAction: The LDRRMFIP was prepared which includes the PPAs chargeable against the current and previous year’s unexpended balances.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal Assessor to:
a. continue the coordination with the Provincial Assessor on the preparation and submission of the Schedule of Fair Market Values of land, buildings and other structures, machinery and other improvements; and

The Municipal Assessor continually assessed using the 2012 general revision.

The MPDC reviewed the organizational and the list of projects to be implemented.

The Municipality identified projects to be implemented in various barangays.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to propose doable and effective measures to resolve delays in liquidation such as
sending of demand letters to concerned officers and employees who did not
liquidate their cash advances within the prescribed period with a reminder that
their salaries could be withheld should they still fail to liquidate after demand.

payment of consultancy services, in accordance with Section 9.1.2 of COA
Circular No. 2012-001.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor to instruct the:

observations.managementAction:
The property records were maintained by the Municipal Accountant and MGSO.

The Municipal Mayor issued Executive Order Nos. EO-RBC 032-2022, 033-2022 and 089-2023 for the creation of Inventory, Appraisal Committee and Disposal Committee.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
a. Municipal Treasurer to:
➢ furnish the Municipal Accountant at the beginning of the year a duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectable for the year which will serve as basis to draw a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) in recording the debit to RPT Receivable/SET Receivable and credit Deferred RPT Income/Deferred SET Income; and
➢ generate/prepare the Certified Statement of RPT/SET Delinquencies as of the given date and reconcile with the Municipal Accountant to ascertain the balances of the RPT Receivables, SET Receivables and the corresponding Deferred RPT Income/Deferred SET Income.
b. Municipal Accountant to reconcile the RPT/SET Receivables accounts with the Municipal Treasurer’s records and make necessary adjustments to present fairly the financial statements.
observations.managementAction:
The duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectable for the year was submitted by the Municipal Treasurer to the Municipal Accountant.

The Municipal Accountant reconciled with the records of the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Assessor.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
a. MGSO to maintain SCs for each type of supplies; and
b. Inventory Committee to ensure the conduct of the physical count of supplies and materials at year-end and thereafter, prepare and submit the RPCI as of a given date to report the physical count of supplies by type of inventory.
observations.managementAction:
The MGSO prepared and maintained the SCs for each type of supplies.

The physical count of supplies was conducted and the RPCI was prepared and submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to draw a JEV to transfer the completed projects under the CIP accounts to the respective assets accounts and to record the corresponding provision for depreciation including its prior year’s depreciation, in compliance with Section 50, Chapter 3 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I, COA Circular No. 2015-009 and Paragraphs 4 and 65 of IPSAS 17.
observations.managementAction:
The adjusting entries were drawn to transfer completed projects under CIP accounts to respective assets accounts and to record corresponding depreciation under JEV No. 2023-04-00739 and JEV No. 2023-04-00741 dated April 28, 2023.

Another JEV was drawn to record the corresponding depreciation under JEV No. 2023-04-000740.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to draw JEV by debiting the AP account and crediting the Due to Officers and Employees account in the same amount of P185,691.01 to ensure that accounts are accurately presented in the financial statements; and henceforth, strictly comply with COA Circular No. 2015-009 in the recording of the transactions.
observations.managementAction: The adjusting entry was drawn to reclassify from AP to the Due to Officers and Employees account in the same amount of P185,691.01 under JEV No. 2023-04-00734.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to verify the validity of payables aged two years and more and revert the balances to the claimants or creditors; otherwise, cause the reversion of those payables aged two years or more which are determined to be without valid claimants/creditors, pursuant to Sections 4(6) and 98 of PD No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: The adjusting entries were drawn to revert to the unappropriated surplus the Accounts Payable which has been outstanding for more than two years and determined to be without valid claims upon evaluation of the Municipal Accountant. Correction of entry were recorded under JEV No. 2023-04-00736 and JEV No. 2023-04-000735 both dated April 28, 2023.

The Municipal Treasurer communicated with the payees.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the MGSO and Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) Chairman, to require contractors, upon payment of 15 percent mobilization fee, to submit an irrevocable standby letter of credit of equivalent value from a commercial bank, a bank guarantee or a surety bond, callable upon demand, issued by a surety or insurance company duly licensed by the Insurance Commission and confirmed by the procuring entity, pursuant to Section 4.2 of Annex “E” of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.
observations.managementAction: The advance payment made to the contractor during the year was supported by the advance payment bond.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) to prepare and submit within the government prescribed period a comprehensive list of all on-going and to be implemented government PPAs, as mandated by Section 2.1 of COA Circular No. 2013-004; and

observations.managementAction:
The MPDC submitted the list of projects to be implemented by the Municipality for CY 2023.

The Municipal Engineer provided the notice to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Human Resource Management Officer (MHRMO), in coordination with the Municipal Budget Officer (MBO), to review the organizational structure and staffing pattern of the Municipality taking into consideration its service requirements and financial capability, subject to the minimum standards and guidelines prescribed by the Civil Service Commission.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality reviewed the organizational structure and staffing pattern in which plantilla items were dissolved and new plantilla items were created. Moreso, new appointments were issued during the year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the LDRRMC, through the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer (MDRRMO), to: a. prepare the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Plan (LDRRMP) of the Municipality by focusing on the programs, projects and activities requiring the highest priority in terms of urgency and impact on the Disaster Risk Reduction and Management (DRRM); b. prepare detailed utilization report to monitor the status of the implementation of the PPAs under the LDRRMF; and c. cause the timely implementation of the PPAs to optimize the utilization of the LDRRMF to strengthen its capacity to prepare and manage disaster risks.
observations.managementAction:
The MDRRMO prepared the LDRRMP of the Municipality by focusing on programs, projects and activities requiring the highest priority in terms of urgency and impact on the DRRM.

observations.managementAction:
The LDRRMO prepared the LDRRMFIP upon request of the Audit Team which was subsequently presented and submitted on March 25, 2024.

The PPAs were presented per account titles in the budget code only such as training, travelling, and office supplies, etc. rather than the specific description of the activities. observations.managementAction: The MGSO prepared and maintained the SCs for each type of supplies and submitted the SSMI report.
